description: Add a partial unique index on the jobs table to prevent concurrent active jobs of the same type, add DuplicateJobError exception, update create_job to catch IntegrityError and raise DuplicateJobError, update jobs_worker.start_job to handle DuplicateJobError, and update the route to flash an appropriate message. Add tests covering all new behavior.
steps: 1. Add DuplicateJobError exception class to flask_app/main_app/db/exceptions.py following the existing exception pattern.

2. Add a partial unique index to the JobRecord model in flask_app/main_app/db/models/jobs.py using SQLAlchemy's Index with a WHERE clause filtering on status IN ('pending', 'running'). Add it via __table_args__ on the model. Import Index from sqlalchemy.

3. Update flask_app/main_app/db/services/jobs_service.py: In the create_job function, wrap the db.session.commit() call in a try-except block that catches sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError. On IntegrityError, rollback the session and raise DuplicateJobError with a descriptive message including the job_type. Add appropriate logging.

4. Update flask_app/main_app/new_jobs/jobs_worker.py: In the start_job function, catch DuplicateJobError from the create_job call (in addition to the existing generic Exception catch). Log a warning when a duplicate is detected and re-raise it so the caller (route) can handle it.

5. Update flask_app/main_app/app_routes/new_jobs.py: In the _start_job function, catch DuplicateJobError specifically (before the generic Exception catch) and flash a user-friendly message like 'A job of this type is already running. Please wait for it to complete.' with category 'warning'. Return None so the route redirects to the jobs list.
6. Export DuplicateJobError from flask_app/main_app/db/services/__init__.py so it can be imported by jobs_worker and the route.

7. Add unit tests in tests/unit/db/services/test_jobs_service.py: (a) Test that creating a second job of the same type while one is 'pending' raises DuplicateJobError. (b) Test that creating a second job of the same type while one is 'running' raises DuplicateJobError. (c) Test that creating a job of the same type after the first is 'completed' succeeds. (d) Test that creating a job of the same type after the first is 'cancelled' succeeds. (e) Test that creating a job of a different type while one is active succeeds.

8. Add a unit test in tests/unit/new_jobs/test_jobs_worker.py: Test that start_job raises DuplicateJobError when create_job raises DuplicateJobError (mock create_job to raise DuplicateJobError and verify it propagates).
9. Verify all existing tests still pass by running: python -m pytest tests/ -v
acceptance_criteria: JobRecord model has a partial unique index on job_type WHERE status IN ('pending', 'running')
create_job raises DuplicateJobError when a duplicate active job is detected via IntegrityError
start_job in jobs_worker propagates DuplicateJobError to callers
The _start_job route handler catches DuplicateJobError and flashes a user-friendly warning
Unit tests cover: duplicate pending, duplicate running, different type allowed, completed allows re-creation, cancelled allows re-creation
All existing tests continue to pass
findings: Tests share a session-scoped SQLite DB without per-test cleanup (no _clean_db fixture in unit tests). Integration tests have a _clean_db fixture but unit tests do not. To avoid conflicts with the new partial unique index, each test must use a unique job_type string. Updated existing tests in test_job_lifecycle_updates.py to use unique job_type names and made MockWorker accept a configurable job_type parameter. Integration tests that created multiple jobs of the same type were updated to complete the first job before creating the second.
